message:
fix: i18n hardcoded English strings across cluster admin/groups/rename dialogs (#8932)

Adds translation keys and wires t() calls in 7 components that were
displaying hardcoded English regardless of the selected language:

- RenameModal: title, current/new-name labels, hint, buttons, validation
  errors ("Name cannot be empty", "contain spaces", "is unchanged",
  "Failed to rename context")
- ConnectTab: success message "Connected — Kubernetes <version>"
  collapsed into a single interpolated key
- ClusterAdmin: page title, subtitle, empty state, error heading, and
  stat sublabels (total/healthy/degraded/offline)
- ClusterGroups: "New Group" button, name placeholder, "Select clusters
  for this group", Cancel/Create buttons, delete tooltip, header count
- Clusters: corrupted sort-preference warning toast and the
  "Local agent not connected" rename error
- ClusterDetailModal: AI mission title and description strings for the
  Diagnose and Repair actions

All new keys live under web/src/locales/en/common.json under the
existing cluster/clusters.groups sections plus a new clusterAdmin
section. RenameModal test updated to match the new translation keys
(the existing useTranslation mock returns the key verbatim).

Fixes #8918, Fixes #8919, Fixes #8920, Fixes #8921, Fixes #8922, Fixes #8923, Fixes #8925, Fixes #8926

Signed-off-by: Andy Anderson <andy@clubanderson.com>

files.patch:
@@ -178,8 +178,8 @@ export function ClusterDetailModal({ clusterName, clusterUser, onClose, onRename
     ].slice(0, 10).join('\n')
 
     startMission({
-      title: `Diagnose ${clusterName.split('/').pop()}`,
-      description: `Analyzing cluster health and identifying issues`,
+      title: t('cluster.diagnoseMissionTitle', { cluster: clusterName.split('/').pop() }),
+      description: t('cluster.diagnoseMissionDescription'),
       type: 'troubleshoot',
       cluster: clusterName,
       initialPrompt: `Analyze the health of Kubernetes cluster "${clusterName}" and identify any issues that need attention.
@@ -216,8 +216,8 @@ Please analyze this cluster and provide:
     ].join('\n')
 
     startMission({
-      title: `Repair ${clusterName.split('/').pop()}`,
-      description: `Automatically fixing cluster issues`,
+      title: t('cluster.repairMissionTitle', { cluster: clusterName.split('/').pop() }),
+      description: t('cluster.repairMissionDescription'),
       type: 'repair',
       cluster: clusterName,
       initialPrompt: `I need help repairing issues in Kubernetes cluster "${clusterName}".
